description: Add a new aircraft at a specific parking space
parameters: rules - V or I (VFR or IFR) weight - S, M, L, or H, (Small, Small+, Large, Heavy) engine - P, T, J, H (Prop, Turboprop, Jet, Helo) space - The parking space to add the aircraft to. type - Overrides a randomly generated aircraft type. [optional] airline - Overrides a randomly generated airline. [optional]
examples: ADD V S P @GA1 ADD I H J @B3 B738 ADD I S H @C27 C402 *KAP

description: Add a new aircraft on a specified bearing, distance and altitude from the airport
parameters: rules - V or I (VFR or IFR) weight - S, M, L, or H, (Small, Small+, Large, Heavy) engine - P, T, J, H (Prop, Turboprop, Jet, Helo) bearing - The bearing from the field on which to add the aircraft. distance - Distance from the threshold in NM.distance - Distance from the field in NM to add the aircraft. altitude - The aircraft will be at this altitude. type - Overrides a randomly generated aircraft type. [optional] airline - Overrides a randomly generated airline. [optional]

description: Add a new aircraft or controller.
parameters: rules - V or I (VFR or IFR) weight - S, M, L, or H, (Small, Small+, Large, Heavy) engine - P, T, J, H (Prop, Turboprop, Jet, Helo) runway - The runway to add the aircraft on approach to. distance - Distance from the threshold in NM. Aircraft spawn lined up on runway if not specified.distance - Distance from the field in NM to add the aircraft. type - Overrides a randomly generated aircraft type. [optional] airline - Overrides a randomly generated airline. [optional]
examples: ADD I L J 33L 20 *AAL ADD V M T 22R 15 BE20 ADD I S P 22R C172

description: Aircraft will break from any taxiing conflicts and will ignore and other traffic on the ground for a short time.
Instructs an aircraft to cross a runway or taxiway that it is currently holding short of, or has previously been instructed to hold short of or the current hold short point if already holding short.
parameters: runway/taxiway - The runway or taxiway to cross.

description: Instructs an aircraft to wait for a different aircraft to pass before continuing after a hold short point.
parameters: aircraft - The callsign of the aircraft to give way to. runway/taxiway - The runway/taxiway to give way at. Will default to the first hold short point if not specified.

description: Instructs the aircraft to hold on the ground or in the air at a certain point.
examples: HOLD HOLD ROBUC 120 HOLD ROBUC 120 10 LEFT

description: Instructs an aircraft to hold short of the specified taxiway/runway
parameters: taxiway/runway - The taxiway/runway to hold short of.

description: Instructs an aircraft to push back on to the specified taxiway. The aircraft will taxi on a heading perpendicular to the taxiway then stop.
parameters: taxiway/spot - The taxiway or spot to push back on to. Optional, aircraft will push straight back if left blank.

description: Instructs an aircraft to resume taxiing or cross a runway if holding short.
A variation of the TAXI command that is more like taxi instruction phraesology. The keyword TAXI is optional within the command.
parameters: runway - The runway to taxi to. taxi-path - List of taxiways and/or runways to taxi via. hold-short-list - List of ruwnays or taxiways to hold short of. It is not required to specify the departure runway.
examples: RWY 27 K C D HS 33L RWY 4R TAXI E B

description: Instructs an aircraft to taxi along the specified path including any hold short points.
parameters: taxi-path - List of taxiways and/or runways to taxi via, optionally ending with a departure runway, parking space or spot. hold-short-list - List of ruwnays or taxiways to hold short of. It is not required to specify the departure runway.
examples: TAXI K C D 27 HS 33L TAXI C A @B3

description: Taxis all parked and loaded aircraft along the specified taxi route. This command executes the TAXI command for each aircraft.
parameters: taxi-path - List of taxiways and/or runways to taxi via, optionally ending with a departure runway, parking space or spot. hold-short-list - List of ruwnays or taxiways to hold short of. It is not required to specify the departure runway.

description: Clears an aircraft for takeoff. This command can be issued when the aircraft is holding short, in position, or any time during taxi to the runway. If no heading is specified the aircraft will fly the SID in their flight plan. If the SID does not exist or there is no SID present, the aircraft will fly runway heading.
parameters: heading - Initial departure heading.

description: Cancels take off clearance. The aircraft will be put into a taxiing state and instructed to hold.
Clears an aircraft for takeoff and instructs the aircraft to make left closed traffic.
Clears an aircraft for takeoff and instructs the aircraft to make right closed traffic.
Clears a helicopter for takeoff from present position.
Clears a helicopter for takeoff on runway heading.
Instructs an aircraft to exit left when able.
Instructs an aircraft to exit right when able.
Instructs an aircraft to exit at a specific taxiway/runway. If the aircraft can not slow down in time, the aircraft will vacate at the next available taxiway.
parameters: taxiway/runway - The taxiway/runway to exit at.

description: Instructs an aircraft to make a full stop.

Instructs an aircraft to go around. Pattern aircraft will make another lap in the pattern. All other aircraft will fly runway heading and maintain either pattern altitude or their present altitude, whichever is higher.
Instructs an aircraft to start its takeoff roll when stopped on the runway during stop-and-go operations.

Instructs an aircraft to make a low approach. The aircraft will overfly the runway at 200 feet AGL and remain in the pattern. The aircraft will continue performing low approaches until instructed otherwise.
Instructs an aircraft to land and hold short of the specified runway.
parameters: runway - The crossing runway to hold short of.

description: Instructs a helicopter to land at present position or at the specified parking spot.
parameters: parking-spot - The parking space to land at. [optional]

description: Instructs an aircraft to line up and wait (position and hold)

Instructs an aircraft to make a stop-and-go. If a number of seconds is specified, the aircraft will stop on the runway and hold for that many seconds before rolling again. Otherwise, it will stop on the runway and wait indefinitely for a 'go' command. The aircraft will continue with stop-and-go operations until instructed otherwise.
Instructs an aircraft to make a touch-and-go. The aircraft will continue with touch-and-go operations until instructed otherwise.
Instructs the selected aircraft to enter final for the specified runway.
parameters: runway - The runway to enter final for.

description: Instructs an aircraft to enter a left base for the specified runway.
parameters: runway - The runway for which to enter the pattern on the left base.

description: Instructs an aircraft to enter a left crosswind for the specified runway.
parameters: runway - The runway for which to enter the pattern on the left crosswind.

description: Instructs an aircraft to enter a left downwind for the specified runway.
parameters: runway - The runway for which to enter the pattern on the left downwind.

description: Instructs an aircraft to enter a right base for the specified runway.
parameters: runway - The runway for which to enter the pattern on the right base.

description: Instructs an aircraft to enter a right crosswind for the specified runway.
parameters: runway - The runway for which to enter the pattern on the right crosswind.

description: Instructs an aircraft to enter a right downwind for the specified runway.
parameters: runway - The runway for which to enter the pattern on the right downwind.

description: Instructs an aircraft to extend current pattern leg. This command will extend the leg indefinitely until the appropriate instruction is issued.
Instructs an aircraft to make a 270 degree turn (instead of a 90 degree turn) when turning to the next leg of its pattern.
Instructs an aircraft to make one or more left 360 degree turn(s), then resume current heading/path.
parameters: number - Number of 360s to complete.

description: Instructs an aircraft to make s-turns on final. The initial turn will be to the left.
parameters: turn-count - The number of full S-Turns to complete. [optional]

description: Instructs an aircraft to make left traffic. Can only be issued during takeoff or to change next pattern direction.
Instructs an aircraft to make a normal approach if previously instructed to make a short approach.
Instructs an aircraft to make one or more right 360 degree turn(s), then resume current heading/path.
parameters: number - Number of 360s to complete.

description: Instructs an aircraft to make s-turns on final. The initial turn will be to the right.
parameters: turn-count - The number of full S-Turns to complete. [optional]

description: Instructs an aircraft to make right traffic. Can only be issued during takeoff or to change next pattern direction.
Instructs an aircraft to make a short approach. The aircraft will fly direct to the threshold instead of flying a square downwind-to-base-to-final pattern.
Cancels a previous instruction to make a 270 degree turn to the next pattern leg.

Assigns a non-standard pattern size to the aircraft. The default pattern size  is specified in the airport file. If you would like the aircraft to fly a smaller or larger pattern, use this command. The size of the pattern dictates how far the downwind is from the runway, and how long the final leg will be.
parameters: size - Pattern size in nautical miles. Fractional values acceptable. Minimum 0.5, maximuim 20.

description: Instructs an aircraft on the downwind to turn base. The aircraft must have passed the threshold in the downwind.
Instructs an aircraft on the upwind to turn crosswind. The aircraft must be passed midfield.
Instructs an aircraft on the crosswind to turn downwind.
Changes the primary airport of an aircraft.
Clears aircraft for the approach from present heading or current path if they intersect.
parameters: approach - The name of the approach or the runway if the approach is the primary ILS approach. Optional if the aircraft or scenario has a predefined PrimaryApproach. airport - The FAA identifier for the airport to clear the aircraft for approach to. This will change the primary reference airport for this aircraft.
examples: CAPP CAPP I04R CAPP 4R CAPP I22L BOS

description: Instructs the aircraft to cross fix or other type of waypoint at a specified altitude. The aircraft must be already path finding to the specified waypoint.
parameters: waypoint - A waypoint along the aircraft's current path. altitude - The altitude to cross the waypoint at in feet or hundreds of feet. speed - The speed to cross the waypoint at.

description: Instructs the aircraft to climb or descend to the specified altitude.
parameters: altitude - Altitude in feet or hundreds of feet.

description: Instructs an aircraft to proceed direct to a waypoint. If the waypoint is on the aircraft's current route it will skip all waypoints prior to the specified waypoint and keep the rest of the path.
parameters: waypoints - The waypoint or waypoints to proceed direct to.

description: Instructs the aircraft to depart a waypoint on a specific heading.
parameters: waypoint - The waypoint on the current path to fly the specified heading after crossing. heading - The heading to fly after crossing over the specified waypoint.

description: Instructs an aircraft to climb/descend via the current path. An altitude can be specified to stop early at. Can be combine with CFIX and/or SPD to alter altitude and speed crossing restrictions.
examples: DVIA DVIA 80 DVIA CFIX ROBUC 100 DVIA SPD 230 GOSHI

description: Instructs an aircraft to climb/descend via the current path. An altitude can be specified to stop early at. Can be combine with CFIX and/or SPD to alter altitude and speed crossing restrictions.
parameters: altitude - The altitude to cross the waypoint at in feet or hundreds of feet.
examples: DVIA DVIA 80 DVIA CFIX ROBUC 100 DVIA SPD 230 GOSHI

description: Instructs an aircraft to climb/descend via the current path. An altitude can be specified to stop early at. Can be combine with CFIX and/or SPD to alter altitude and speed crossing restrictions.
parameters: waypoint - A waypoint along the aircraft's current path. altitude - The altitude to cross the waypoint at in feet or hundreds of feet.
examples: DVIA DVIA 80 DVIA CFIX ROBUC 100 DVIA SPD 230 GOSHI

description: Instructs an aircraft to climb/descend via the current path. An altitude can be specified to stop early at. Can be combine with CFIX and/or SPD to alter altitude and speed crossing restrictions.
parameters: speed - The speed to cross the waypoint at. waypoint - A waypoint along the aircraft's current path.
examples: DVIA DVIA 80 DVIA CFIX ROBUC 100 DVIA SPD 230 GOSHI

description: Instructs an aircraft to expedite climb/descent. An optional altitude can be defined to resume normal operations at.
parameters: altitude - The altitude to resume normal climb/descent rates.

description: Instructs the aircraft to hold on the ground or in the air at a certain point.
parameters: waypoint - The waypoint to hold at. course - The inbound course of the hold. distance - The leg distance in NM. direction - The direction of turns in the hold. Accepts L, LEFT, R or RIGHT.
examples: HOLD HOLD ROBUC 120 HOLD ROBUC 120 10 LEFT

description: Instructs an aircraft to join a STAR with an optional transition. The aircraft must already be direct to a waypoint on the arrival. The aircraft will not descend automatically.
parameters: star - The STAR to join along the aircraft's current path. transition - The runway transition on the STAR.

description: Instructs the aircraft to join the specified airway from the assigned heading. Aircraft will join the airway at the intersection then continue in that direction.
parameters: airway - The airway to join

description: Instructs the aircraft to join the final approach course from an intercept heading or if already direct to a waypoint on the approach.
parameters: approach - The name of the approach or the runway if the approach is the primary ILS approach.

description: Instructs the aircraft to join the final approach course from an intercept heading or if already direct to a waypoint on the approach.
parameters: approach - The name of the approach or the runway if the approach is the primary ILS approach. Optional if the aircraft or scenario has a predefined PrimaryApproach.

description: Instructs an aircraft to resume normal climb/descent rate after being told to expedite.
Instructs an aircraft to maintain the specified mach.
parameters: mach - The mach to maintain with an optional decimal point.

description: Instructs an aircraft to reduce to an automatically generated final approach speed.
Instructs an aircraft to ignore any previous speed restrictions.

Instructs the aircraft to increase/decrease to the specified speed. SPD 0 will remove any previous speed restrictions and the aircraft will automatically adjust speed. A termination waypoint can be used to cancel speed restictions at.
parameters: speed - The speed to maintain. 0 indicates normal speed. termination - The waypoint to resume normal speed at. The aircraft must be navigating to this waypoint. [optional]

description: Instructs an aircraft to fly a specific heading.
parameters: heading - The new heading to fly.

description: Instructs an aircraft to maintain its present heading.
Left turn a specific number of degrees relative to current heading.
parameters: degrees - Number of degrees to turn to the left relative to current heading.

description: Right turn a specific number of degrees relative to current heading.
parameters: degrees - Number of degrees to turn to the right relative to current heading.

description: Instructs the aircraft to turn the specified number of degrees in the specified direction.
parameters: degrees - The number of degrees to turn in the specified direction. direction - The direction to turn. Accepts L, LEFT, R or RIGHT

description: Instructs an aircraft to turn left to the specified heading.
parameters: heading - The new heading to fly. The aircraft will turn left regardless of which direction is shorter.

description: Instructs an aircraft to turn right to the specified heading.
parameters: heading - The new heading to fly. The aircraft will turn right regardless of which direction is shorter.

description: Instructs aircraft to squawk ident.
Assigns a random squawk code to all aircraft in the scenario and makes them squawk that code.
Instructs an aircraft to squawk normal (Mode C).
Sets all the transponder of all aircraft to normal (Mode C).
Instructs an aircraft to squawk the specified transponder code. If no code is specified the aircraft will squawk the atc assigned code.
parameters: code - The new transponder code.

description: Instructs all aircraft to squawk their assigned code.
Instructs an aircraft to squawk the specified transponder code and ident. If no code is specified the aircraft will squawk the atc assigned code.
parameters: code - The new transponder code.

description: Instructs the aircraft to squawk VFR (1200).
Instructs the aircraft to squawk standby.
Sets all the transponder of all aircraft to standby.
Makes the aircraft complete a command at a waypoint, altitude or speed.
parameters: location - A waypoint along the current path, altitude greater than 1000 feet, or knots in airspeed to complete the command at. command - A full command to complete at the location.
examples: AT MINNK FH 030 AT 5000 SPD 210 AT 210 DM 5000

description: Indicates that the aircraft has a clearance within the application window.
Deletes currently selected aircraft.
Removes all queued commands for the selected aircraft.
Creates an IFR flight plan for the aircraft. This can be used if the instructor wishes to have a specific flight plan for an aircraft after it is created with one of the 'ADD' commands.
parameters: type - The new aircraft type. altitude - The new cruising altitude. route - The new route. Use spaces to separate waypoints. The first entry must be the departure airport. The last entry must be the arrival airport.
examples: FP B738 220 KBOS SSOXS6 SSOXS BUZRD SEY PARCH3 KJFK FP B190 160 KBOS BOSOX ALB KALB

description: Runs the specified command when a handoff is accepted by ATCTrainer ATC.
parameters: command - A full command to execute when the handoff is accepted.
examples: ONHO QZ 240 ONHO SPD 210 ONHO CM 340

description: Opens a chat window to private message the specified controller.
parameters: controller - The callsign of the controller to open a chat with.

description: Shows statistics on the number of arrivals and departures, including totals and per-minute averages.
Pauses the simulation.
Alters the flight plan remarks for an aircraft. The aircraft will have to refile the initial flight plan data or data provided with the FP or VP commands.
parameters: text - The text to enter in the aircraft's flight strip remarks.
examples: REMARKS First flight on vatsim. How do I fly? REMARKS NO SID

description: Sends the specified message on the frequency. Best used in preset commands to provide information to the instructor. Can be used in conjunction with the DELAY or AT command.
parameters: message - The message to send.

description: Sends the specified message over the command frequency. Output will never be sent to the command line and always to the frequency. Useful for simulating text pilots.
parameters: message - The message to send.

description: Outputs any pending 'AT' commands that have not executed.
Increases the simulation rate to the set amount. Increasing simulation rate may lead to increased CPU usage. Use at your own risk.
parameters: rate - The simulation rate to set. Value must be 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16.

description: Unpauses the simulation.
Creates a VFR flight plan for the aircraft. This can be used if the instructor wishes to have a specific flight plan for an aircraft after it is created with one of the 'ADD' commands.
parameters: type - The new aircraft type. altitude - The new cruising altitude. route - The new route. Use spaces to separate waypoints. The first entry must be the departure airport. The last entry must be the arrival airport.
examples: VP C172/G KBOS DCT GPS KALB VP P28A 5500 KBOS MANCH LBNON BTV KBTV

description: Adds a command with a specified delay in seconds before it is executed.
parameters: delay - Time in seconds to delay the execution of the specified command. command - The command to execute.

description: Accept a pending handoff of an aircraft.
Accept all pending handoffs to controllers
Annotates all strips for the defined aircraft.
parameters: boxNumber - The box number to enter the annotation in. Must be between 0 and 9 inclusive. value - The value to enter into the box.

description: Cancels a pending handoff
Sets the temporary altitude for the specified aircraft.
parameters: altitude - The altitude to set as the cruize altitude. For ERAM tracked aircraft, this value could be VFR, OTP, a block altitude, or any other QZ accepted value.

description: Drops track on the selected aircraft.
Hands off the selected or defined aircraft to the student position or the specified controller
parameters: handoffId - The handoff ID of the controller to handoff the aircraft to. If not specified defaults to the student's position

description: Hands off all ATCTrainer generated aircraft to the student's position or the defined controller.
parameters: handoffId - The handoff ID of the controller to handoff the aircraft to. If not specified defaults to the student's position

description: Runs /OK on the specified aircraft. This command only works with ERAM positions.
parameters: handoffId - Handoff ID of the target position. This position must be an ERAM position.

description: Sends a point out for the target aircraft to a specific controller.
parameters: handoffId - Callsign of the controller to point out to

description: Sets the scratchpad of the specified aircraft
parameters: scratchpad - The scratchpad text to enter.

description: Pushes all flight strips for the aircraft to the defined flight strip bay.
parameters: bay - The name of the strip bay to push to. Must match the name entered in the facility flight strip configuration.

description: Sets the temporary altitude for the specified aircraft.
parameters: altitude - The altitude to set in the temp alt field.

description: Tracks the selected aircraft with the specified position.
parameters: handoffId - Handoff ID of the target position

description: Displays the names of all available approaches.
The aircraft will instantaneously go to the specified altitude.
parameters: altitude - Altitude in feet or hundreds of feet.

description: Instructs the aircraft to instantaneously fly the specific heading.
parameters: heading - The new heading to fly.

description: ATCTrainer will delete any aircraft with the callsign 'WPT#' removing a previously shown path.
ATCTrainer will generate aircraft with the callsign 'WPT#' at all of the waypoints in the selected aircraft's path
Instructs the aircraft to instantaneously increase/decrease to the specified speed.
parameters: speed - The speed to maintain.
